name: Beetroot Raita Recipe (Beet Raita)
ingredients_json: [{"raw_text": "100 grams beetroots (or 1 medium-sized beetroot, peeled and cut into ½ inch cubes)", "source_position": 1}, {"raw_text": "¼ cup water (plus 2 cups for boiling the beets)", "source_position": 2}, {"raw_text": "2 cups chilled plain yogurt (dahi, curd)", "source_position": 3}, {"raw_text": "½ teaspoon salt (or to taste)", "source_position": 4}, {"raw_text": "1 teaspoon roasted cumin powder", "source_position": 5}, {"raw_text": "2 teaspoons finely chopped green chilies (or to taste)", "source_position": 6}, {"raw_text": "2 tablespoons oil", "source_position": 7}, {"raw_text": "½ teaspoon brown mustard seeds", "source_position": 8}, {"raw_text": "10-12 whole curry leaves", "source_position": 9}, {"raw_text": "2 whole dry red chilies", "source_position": 10}, {"raw_text": "2 tablespoons crushed raw peanuts", "source_position": 11}]
cook_steps: [{"step_number": 1, "instruction": "Wash 100 g (1 medium-sized) beetroot and peel it using a vegetable peeler. Chop off ½-inch pieces from the top and bottom and cut the beetroots into ½-inch cubes."}, {"step_number": 2, "instruction": "Tip – Be cautious when handling beets. They leave permanent marks on the clothes and color the surfaces that come into contact with them. Use an old chopping board and knife, and wear an apron. Chop the green chilies."}, {"step_number": 3, "instruction": "Heat 2 cups of water in a pan over medium-high heat. Once the water comes to a boil, add the beetroots. Cover the pan with a lid and cook until they are softened (8-10 minutes)."}, {"step_number": 4, "instruction": "Note – You can also cook the beetroot in a traditional pressure cooker or instant pot. I am sharing the methods in notes, just in case you want to check."}, {"step_number": 5, "instruction": "Drain the water and add the beetroot to a blender along with ¼ cup of cold water. Blend to make a smooth puree."}, {"step_number": 6, "instruction": "Tip – You can use the leftover water from boiling the beets to make curries or knead the dough."}, {"step_number": 7, "instruction": "Add yogurt to a bowl and whisk it until it is smooth and creamy."}, {"step_number": 8, "instruction": "Add the beetroot puree, salt, cumin powder, and green chilies to the bowl and mix well."}, {"step_number": 9, "instruction": "Note – You can add some water to thin the raita if it is thick for your liking. The ideal consistency should be like that of custard."}, {"step_number": 10, "instruction": "Heat oil in a small skillet over medium-high heat."}, {"step_number": 11, "instruction": "Once the oil is hot, add mustard seeds and curry leaves and let them crackle for 4-5 seconds."}, {"step_number": 12, "instruction": "Add dry red chilies and lightly crushed peanuts to the skillet and fry until they are crispy and golden brown in color."}, {"step_number": 13, "instruction": "Pour the tempering over the beet raita and stir well. Serve chilled."}, {"step_number": 14, "instruction": "Tip – Save some tempering for garnishing."}]

name: Paneer Masala Recipe

cook_steps: [{"step_number": 1, "instruction": "Rinse 14 oz (400 g) of paneer slabs and cut them into 1-inch pieces. Tip – If using store-bought paneer, then soak it in hot water for 10 minutes to make it soft."}, {"step_number": 2, "instruction": "Mix the paneer pieces with the following ingredients in a large mixing bowl and keep aside for 15 minutes."}, {"step_number": 3, "instruction": "Rinse paneer slabs and cut them into 1-inch pieces."}, {"step_number": 4, "instruction": "Mix the paneer pieces with salt, chili powder, and turmeric powder in a large mixing bowl and keep aside for 15 minutes."}, {"step_number": 5, "instruction": "Heat oil in a pan over medium-high heat."}, {"step_number": 6, "instruction": "Arrange the paneer on the pan in a single layer and fry from all sides until browned."}, {"step_number": 7, "instruction": "Note – Fry in batches if the paneer pieces are not fitting in a single layer."}, {"step_number": 8, "instruction": "Remove the paneer from the pan and keep it aside."}, {"step_number": 9, "instruction": "Add oil and ghee to the same pan and heat over medium-high heat."}, {"step_number": 10, "instruction": "Once the oil is hot, add cardamoms, cloves, black peppercorns, cinnamon, dry red chilies, cumin seeds, and asafetida, and fry for 5-6 seconds."}, {"step_number": 11, "instruction": "Add onions and fry until it turns slightly brown (6-8 minutes), stirring frequently."}, {"step_number": 12, "instruction": "Add ginger garlic paste and fry until the onions are nicely browned (6-8 minutes), stirring frequently."}, {"step_number": 13, "instruction": "Now add coriander powder, turmeric powder, red chili powder, cumin powder, garam masala powder, besan, and, salt, and fry for 10-12 seconds."}, {"step_number": 14, "instruction": "Add tomatoes and green chilies and cook until the oil starts to separate from the sides of the pan (3-4 minutes), stirring frequently."}, {"step_number": 15, "instruction": "Now add 1 cup of water, fried paneer pieces, and Kasuri methi, and mix well. Stir continuously to prevent the milk from scorching at the bottom of the pan."}, {"step_number": 4, "instruction": "Once the milk comes to a boil, add nut powder, sugar, cardamom powder, turmeric powder, milk powder, black pepper powder, and nutmeg powder to the pan and mix well."}, {"step_number": 5, "instruction": "Reduce the heat to low and cook the milk for 5-6 minutes, stirring frequently."}, {"step_number": 6, "instruction": "Check for sugar and add more if required."}, {"step_number": 7, "instruction": "Add 2-3 drops of kewda essence and mix well. Serve hot."}]

name: Gajar Halwa Recipe (Carrot Halwa)
ingredients_json: [{"raw_text": "500 grams red carrots", "source_position": 1}, {"raw_text": "2 cups whole milk (full-fat milk)", "source_position": 2}, {"raw_text": "60 grams ghee", "source_position": 3}, {"raw_text": "120 grams sugar", "source_position": 4}, {"raw_text": "120 grams khoya (mawa) (grated)", "source_position": 5}, {"raw_text": "1 teaspoon cardamom powder", "source_position": 6}, {"raw_text": "4 tablespoons slivered nuts (I used almonds and pistachios)", "source_position": 7}]
cook_steps: [{"step_number": 1, "instruction": "Wash the carrots with water and peel them using a vegetable peeler."}, {"step_number": 2, "instruction": "Then, grate the peeled carrots using the medium-holed box grater. Discard the top, which is hard to grate. You can also use a food processor fitted with a grater attachment to do this."}, {"step_number": 3, "instruction": "Add grated red carrots and milk to a heavy-bottomed pan and mix well."}, {"step_number": 4, "instruction": "Cook the mixture on medium-low heat until all the milk is absorbed, stirring frequently. It will take about 20-25 minutes."}, {"step_number": 5, "instruction": "Once the milk is absorbed and the carrot mixture looks thick, add ghee to the pan and cook for 10-12 minutes, stirring frequently."}, {"step_number": 6, "instruction": "Next, add sugar to the pan and cook for an additional 4-5 minutes."}, {"step_number": 7, "instruction": "Note – You can increase or decrease the amount of sugar as per your liking. I like very lightly sweet halwa and this measurement is perfect for that."}, {"step_number": 8, "instruction": "Once the sugar has dissolved well in the halwa, add khoya to the pan and cook for an additional 25-30 minutes, until the ghee starts to separate from the sides of the pan. Continue to stir the mixture frequently throughout the cooking process."}, {"step_number": 9, "instruction": "Add cardamom powder and slivered almonds and pistachios and mix well."}, {"step_number": 10, "instruction": "Serve hot."}]

name: Rava Idli Recipe (Semolina Idli)
ingredients_json: [{"raw_text": "2 tablespoons oil", "source_position": 1}, {"raw_text": "½ teaspoon brown mustard seeds", "source_position": 2}, {"raw_text": "½ teaspoon cumin seeds", "source_position": 3}, {"raw_text": "1 teaspoon chana dal", "source_position": 4}, {"raw_text": "¼ teaspoon asafetida (hing)", "source_position": 5}, {"raw_text": "10-12 curry leaves", "source_position": 6}, {"raw_text": "1 teaspoon grated ginger", "source_position": 7}, {"raw_text": "1 teaspoon finely chopped green chilies", "source_position": 8}, {"raw_text": "1 dry red chili (broken into small pieces)", "source_position": 9}, {"raw_text": "4-5 cashew nuts (crushed)", "source_position": 10}, {"raw_text": "1 cup barik rava (fine semolina)", "source_position": 11}, {"raw_text": "1 teaspoon salt", "source_position": 12}, {"raw_text": "½ cup plain yogurt (dahi, curd)", "source_position": 13}, {"raw_text": "1 tablespoon chopped cilantro (fresh coriander leaves)", "source_position": 14}, {"raw_text": "1 teaspoon Eno Fruit Salt", "source_position": 15}]
cook_steps: [{"step_number": 1, "instruction": "Heat 2 tablespoon oil in a pan over medium heat."}, {"step_number": 2, "instruction": "Once the oil is hot, add ½ teaspoon of brown mustard seeds, ½ teaspoon of cumin seeds, and 1 teaspoon of chana dal, and fry for 6-8 seconds."}, {"step_number": 3, "instruction": "Add the following ingredients and fry until the cashew nuts turn slightly brown."}, {"step_number": 4, "instruction": "1 teaspoon finely chopped green chilies"}, {"step_number": 5, "instruction": "Reduce the heat to low."}, {"step_number": 6, "instruction": "Add 1 cup of fine rava and roast for 30-40 seconds, stirring continuously."}, {"step_number": 7, "instruction": "Remove the pan from the heat and let the rava cool completely."}, {"step_number": 8, "instruction": "Tip: Always roast the rava on low heat until it becomes aromatic. Don’t make it brown! Also, never roast it on high heat, as the rava might burn."}, {"step_number": 9, "instruction": "Heat oil in a pan over medium heat."}, {"step_number": 10, "instruction": "Once the oil is hot, add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, and chana dal, and fry for 6-8 seconds."}, {"step_number": 11, "instruction": "Add asafetida, curry leaves, ginger, green chilies, dry red chili, cashew nuts, and fry until the cashew nuts turn slightly brown."}, {"step_number": 12, "instruction": "Add rava and roast for 30-40 seconds, stirring continuously."}, {"step_number": 13, "instruction": "Transfer the cooled roasted rava to a mixing bowl."}, {"step_number": 14, "instruction": "Add salt, yogurt, cilantro, and ½ cup of water to the bowl and mix until well combined."}, {"step_number": 15, "instruction": "Mix the batter thoroughly. Check the consistency of the batter once again. Add more water if required. The consistency of the batter should be thick yet droppable, similar to the consistency of regular idli batter."}, {"step_number": 16, "instruction": "Add Eno fruit salt and mix well again."}, {"step_number": 17, "instruction": "Add 2 cups of water to the idli pan and heat over high heat. Let the water come to a boil."}, {"step_number": 18, "instruction": "Pour the batter into the greased molds and keep the molds in the pan."}, {"step_number": 19, "instruction": "Steam the idlis for 12 minutes over high heat."}, {"step_number": 20, "instruction": "Remove the idlis from the molds using a spoon and serve them hot."}]

name: Papdi Chaat Recipe (Papri Chaat)
ingredients_json: [{"raw_text": "20-25 pieces papdi", "source_position": 1}, {"raw_text": "1 and ½ cups plain yogurt (dahi, curd) (chilled)", "source_position": 2}, {"raw_text": "2 teaspoons granulated white sugar", "source_position": 3}, {"raw_text": "½ cup boiled and cubed potatoes", "source_position": 4}, {"raw_text": "½ cup cooked black chickpeas", "source_position": 5}, {"raw_text": "2 tablespoons chopped onions", "source_position": 6}, {"raw_text": "2 tablespoons coriander mint chutney", "source_position": 7}, {"raw_text": "2 tablespoons tamarind chutney", "source_position": 8}, {"raw_text": "¼ teaspoon salt (or to taste)", "source_position": 9}, {"raw_text": "¼ teaspoon red chili powder", "source_position": 10}, {"raw_text": "¼ teaspoon roasted cumin powder", "source_position": 11}, {"raw_text": "½ teaspoon chaat masala powder", "source_position": 12}, {"raw_text": "2 tablespoons chopped cilantro (fresh coriander leaves)", "source_position": 13}, {"raw_text": "¼ cup fine sev", "source_position": 14}, {"raw_text": "2 tablespoons pomegranate seeds", "source_position": 15}]
cook_steps: [{"step_number": 1, "instruction": "Add 1 ½ cups of chilled yogurt and 2 teaspoon of sugar to a medium mixing bowl, and whisk with a wire whisk until the sugar dissolves."}, {"step_number": 2, "instruction": "Note -The consistency of the yogurt and sugar mixture should be like that of a custard, thick but flowing. If the yogurt is thick, then thin it down with some water."}, {"step_number": 3, "instruction": "Arrange 20-25 papdi on a large serving plate (or 10-12 papdi on 2 small plates)."}, {"step_number": 4, "instruction": "Pour the whisked yogurt over the papdi."}, {"step_number": 5, "instruction": "Top with ½ cup of boiled, cubed potatoes and ½ cup of cooked black chickpeas."}, {"step_number": 6, "instruction": "Drizzle 2 tablespoon of coriander-mint chutney and 2 tablespoon of tamarind chutney on top. If the chutneys are not pourable, add some water."}, {"step_number": 7, "instruction": "Sprinkle the following ingredients on top."}, {"step_number": 8, "instruction": "2 tablespoon chopped onions"}, {"step_number": 9, "instruction": "Garnish with the following ingredients."}, {"step_number": 10, "instruction": "2 tablespoon chopped coriander"}, {"step_number": 11, "instruction": "Serve immediately."}, {"step_number": 12, "instruction": "Add yogurt and sugar to a medium size mixing bowl and whisk using a wire whisk until the sugar is dissolved."}, {"step_number": 13, "instruction": "Pour the whisked yogurt on the top of papdi."}, {"step_number": 14, "instruction": "Drizzle coriander mint chutney and tamarind chutney on top. If the chutneys are not of pourable consistency, add some water to them."}, {"step_number": 15, "instruction": "Sprinkle onions, salt, red chili powder, cumin powder, and chaat masala on top."}, {"step_number": 16, "instruction": "Garnish with coriander, pomegranate seeds, and fine sev. Serve immediately."}]

name: Makki ka Dhokla Recipe

cook_steps: [{"step_number": 1, "instruction": "Add the following ingredients to a large bowl."}, {"step_number": 2, "instruction": "2 teaspoon chopped green chilies"}, {"step_number": 3, "instruction": "¼ cup finely chopped radish greens (or methi leaves)"}, {"step_number": 4, "instruction": "¼ cup finely chopped spinach"}, {"step_number": 5, "instruction": "2 tablespoon finely chopped radish"}, {"step_number": 6, "instruction": "Mix the mixture with your fingers until it is crumbly."}, {"step_number": 7, "instruction": "Add lukewarm water, a little at a time , and knead until a soft dough forms. Note – If the dough is hard, the dhokla will turn out dense."}, {"step_number": 8, "instruction": "Divide the dough into 6 equal parts."}, {"step_number": 9, "instruction": "Shape the dough into balls and flatten them a bit. name: Kobbari Pachadi Recipe (Andhra Coconut Chutney)
ingredients_json: [{"raw_text": "2 tablespoons oil", "source_position": 1}, {"raw_text": "2 teaspoons chana dal", "source_position": 2}, {"raw_text": "2 teaspoons white urad dal", "source_position": 3}, {"raw_text": "1 teaspoon coriander seeds", "source_position": 4}, {"raw_text": "2-3 whole dried red chilies (stalks removed)", "source_position": 5}, {"raw_text": "2-3 green chilies (stalks removed)", "source_position": 6}, {"raw_text": "1 teaspoon chopped ginger", "source_position": 7}, {"raw_text": "350 grams sliced fresh coconut", "source_position": 8}, {"raw_text": "1 tablespoons tamarind paste", "source_position": 9}, {"raw_text": "½ teaspoon salt", "source_position": 10}, {"raw_text": "2 teaspoons oil", "source_position": 11}, {"raw_text": "½ teaspoon brown mustard seeds", "source_position": 12}, {"raw_text": "1 dry red chili (broken into small pieces)", "source_position": 13}, {"raw_text": "8-10 curry leaves", "source_position": 14}]
cook_steps: [{"step_number": 1, "instruction": "Heat 2 tablespoon oil in a pan over medium heat."}, {"step_number": 2, "instruction": "Once the oil is hot, add 2 teaspoon chana dal, 2 teaspoon white urad dal, and 1 teaspoon coriander seeds and roast until the lentils turn slightly brown, stirring frequently."}, {"step_number": 3, "instruction": "Add 2-3 whole dried red chilies, 2-3 green chilies, and 1 teaspoon chopped ginger, and saute for 30 seconds."}, {"step_number": 4, "instruction": "Add 350 g of sliced fresh coconut and cook for 5-6 minutes, until lightly browned."}, {"step_number": 5, "instruction": "Remove the pan from the heat and let the coconut cool down for 5 minutes."}, {"step_number": 6, "instruction": "Heat oil in a pan over medium heat."}, {"step_number": 7, "instruction": "Once the oil is hot, add chana dal, white urad dal, and coriander seeds and roast until the lentils turn slightly brown, stirring frequently."}, {"step_number": 8, "instruction": "Add whole dried red chilies, green chilies, and ginger, and saute for 30 seconds."}, {"step_number": 9, "instruction": "Add the sliced fresh coconut and cook for 5-6 minutes, until lightly browned."}, {"step_number": 10, "instruction": "Add the cooled coconut mixture to a high-speed blender."}, {"step_number": 11, "instruction": "Add tamarind paste, salt, and ¾ cup of water, and blend to make a smooth chutney. Add more water if required while blending the chutney."}, {"step_number": 12, "instruction": "Check for salt and tamarind, and add more if needed."}, {"step_number": 13, "instruction": "Heat oil in a small pan over medium heat."}, {"step_number": 14, "instruction": "Once the oil is hot, add brown mustard seeds, dry red chili, and curry leaves.
